question: Blind since shortly after birth, this singer and pianist became one of Motown's biggest stars, recording classics like "Superstition" and "Sir Duke".
This German composer began losing his hearing in early adulthood and composed much of their later work while deaf, including the Ninth Symphony.
This pop superstar has spoken openly about living with fibromyalgia and has incorporated disability imagery into their work, including the wheelchair-centered "Paparazzi" music video.

In Pokémon, Shiny Pokémon are rare color variants, like the red Gyarados shown on the left. 
For people with this type of color blindness, its red scales appear brownish-gray, making it look much closer to the common blue version.
image_alt: Side-by-side comparison of a shiny red Gyarados on the left, and the brown-gray appearance of a blue or red Gyarados on the right for those with the condition in question, illustrating how the rare color variant appears to players with normal color vision

question: Which Pokémon is described by this alt text: A yellow, rodent-like Pokémon with long ears tipped in black and red circular cheek pouches.
Often used to skip through dialogue, this adjustable game setting also helps reduce cognitive load for players with dyslexia and other processing disorders.
image_alt: Pokémon game settings menu with the option in question highlighted, showing slow, mid, and fast settings

question: This painter’s severe cataracts caused blurred yellow, brown, and red vision that influenced their “Water Lilies” series.
image_alt: Oil painting depicting a reflective pond surface covered in floating lily pads and pink and white water lily blooms rendered in soft, blurred brushstrokes. A wooden foot bridge spans the pond.

question: This spoken narration of key visual elements, often provided via headsets or apps, allows blind or low-vision visitors to experience visual art in a gallery or museum setting.
This famous Mexican painter used art to explore their experience with chronic pain and disability following a streetcar accident.

This painting is described by the following alternative text: Portrait of a woman with a subtle smile, wearing a Renaissance-style dress and sitting with her hands folded against a misty landscape background.
This Blackboard product gives content an accessibility score and step-by-step guidance to improve items like color contrast, headings, and alternative text.
In Blackboard, it is more efficient for screen reader users to navigate by these, rather than using the tab key.

*"I have struggled with every other screen capturing program I have tried. Using [this Blackboard product] was the first time I could start and stop a recording without help. It was liberating for me … job well done on this one!"*
–Visually impaired faculty member who uses JAWs to teach fully online.

*When accessibility is everyone’s responsibility, learning together matters.*
This group at Blackboard exists so anyone can get involved, grow their knowledge, and help move accessibility forward.
This invention empowers individuals with mobility limitations, brightens your smile, and makes your hygienist happy!
Making it easier for people to stay hydrated, this product was originally designed with accessibility in mind.

After developing severe carpal tunnel syndrome while pursuing his PhD, Wayne Westerman helped create this widely used technology. Its use helped restore his ability to use a computer effectively and continue his studies.
Which assistive feature do these symbols represent on a mobile device?
image_alt: A row of mobile accessibility icons related to hearing, including the profile view of an ear for Apple products, and an ear with a dot inside and an curved line around the outside of the ear for Android.

question: Which audio feature do these icons represent on a mobile device?
image_alt: Mobile accessibility icons including a figure of a person with an offset speaker symbol on top for Apple, and a pentagon with a word bubble inside for Android.

question: What feature do these icons symbolize on a mobile device?
image_alt: Mobile accessibility icons featuring an eye leading multiple stacked horizontal lines of different lengths for apple, and an eye centered between four square corners for Android.
